prose.overview: Royal Lakeside Golf Club occupies a scenic riverside setting along the Bangpakong River in Chachoengsao Province, roughly an hour southeast of Bangkok via the Bangna-Trat Highway. Opened in 1993 and significantly renovated in 2011 with new ultra-dwarf Bermuda greens, Royal Lakeside has earned a loyal following among Japanese golf tourists and Bangkok-based expatriates who appreciate its combination of good course conditions, friendly caddies, and attractive green fees. The club's distinctive domed clubhouse is a regional landmark, and the riverside atmosphere — with a perimeter lagoon doubling as a water hazard — gives the course a tranquil, resort-like character unusual for a public-access venue. It is particularly popular during the cooler season from November to February.
prose.layout_and_experience: The 18-hole par-72 layout measures 6,815 yards from the blue tees and traces a thoughtful path through the riverside landscape. Sumitomo Construction's design places an emphasis on visual challenge rather than sheer length, using the Bangpakong River and a surrounding perimeter lagoon to create natural out-of-bounds that demand accuracy from the tee on several holes. The renovated greens, constructed in 2011 to ultra-dwarf Bermuda specification, are notably undulating and fast-rolling — among the best putting surfaces in this price bracket. Approach play requires careful club selection as the greens can be deceptively difficult to hold. The par-3 holes are particularly memorable, with water in play on most of them. Fairways are Zoysia grass, holding firm through the dry season. A maximum group size of five on weekdays and five on weekends keeps pace of play reasonable. The setting is genuinely picturesque, with the river visible from multiple holes.
prose.tips: Advance booking is recommended, especially for weekend rounds. The weekday green fee is significantly lower than weekends — plan a mid-week visit if possible. Caddies are used by all players and are knowledgeable about the undulating greens. Bring extra golf balls as the water hazards punish wayward shots. The club is 60 minutes east of Bangkok on the Bangna-Trat Highway (Route 34) — GPS the address at km 51 as signage can be easy to miss. The Japanese restaurant in the clubhouse is a draw in its own right.
prose.location_and_access: Royal Lakeside Golf Club is located at Km 51 of the Bangna-Trat Highway in Tarkham, Bang Pakong, Chachoengsao. It sits approximately 55 km from central Bangkok, about 60 minutes by car. The route follows Bangna-Trat (Route 34) eastbound — the same road used to reach Pattaya. There is no public transit option; a private car or Grab taxi is recommended. The club is also 60 minutes from downtown Pattaya for golfers combining a Bangkok and Pattaya trip.
prose.rental_cta_context: Combining a Bangkok trip with a round at Royal Lakeside? LENGOLF delivers tour-quality clubs to your Bangkok hotel — travel light and still play with the best equipment.
